findings.description: levenshtein() runs unbounded O(len(a)*len(b)) in both time and memory with no length cap; if both arguments are attacker-controlled and unbounded (plausible for a web-service entry point doing fuzzy matching), two large inputs cause algorithmic-complexity CPU/memory DoS (CWE-407 / OWASP A06 Insecure Design).

mask_middle(), a stated data-masking control, reveals all-but-one character for values just above the visible*2 threshold (e.g., a 9-char secret with visible=4 exposes 8 of 9 chars), contradicting its 'avoid leaking most of the content' promise (CWE-200 information disclosure).

mask_middle() emits output whose length always equals the input length (mask_char * len(value)), leaking the exact length of the masked secret/token to anyone who sees the masked form (CWE-200 minor information disclosure).

slugify() applies NFKD normalization then ASCII-ignore, so distinct Unicode inputs (confusables / different normalization forms) can collapse to identical slugs — a collision/spoofing risk only if slugs are later used as unique or security-relevant identifiers (CWE-178/CWE-180, design-context dependent).
